data.title: The Devil's Own Luck
data.premise: You wake up in a filthy London alley with the mother of all hangovers, but something's wrong—the buildings look medieval, everyone's speaking in Shakespearean riddles, and your iPhone is nowhere to be found. Welcome to 1547, where being a confused drunk is the least of your problems when the Inquisition thinks you're a witch.
data.central_question: Can you survive long enough in Tudor England to find a way back to your own time, or will you burn as a heretic?
data.ending_shape: escape to modern times or death in the past
data.healing_context: Wounds heal naturally over days and weeks with rest and crude medical care. Barber-surgeons can set bones and stitch cuts, but infection is a constant threat. Herbal remedies provide minor relief but no miraculous cures. Without proper treatment, even minor injuries can fester and kill.
starting_inventory: empty wallet with credit cards and driver's license
three shillings (found in your pocket somehow)

setting.name: Tudor London, 1547
setting.description: The sprawling, stinking capital of Henry VIII's England, where the Thames runs brown with sewage and the streets echo with the cries of merchants, beggars, and the occasional heretic being dragged to Smithfield. Plague rats scurry between timber-framed houses that lean precariously over cobblestone alleys slick with mud and worse.
setting.tone_notes: Gritty medieval squalor meets dark comedy as modern sensibilities clash with Tudor brutality. The absurdity of the situation provides gallows humor, but the very real danger of disease, violence, and religious persecution keeps the stakes high.

acts.title: Strange New World
acts.description: Joe awakens in Tudor London and must navigate the immediate challenges of survival while trying to understand what happened to him. He encounters suspicious locals, struggles with the language and customs, and attracts unwanted attention from both criminals and authorities.
acts.end_condition: Joe is arrested by the Watch or flees London after a major incident
locked_constraints: Joe cannot learn the full truth about his time displacement yet
No direct confrontation with the Inquisition
Joe cannot find a way home
key_beats: Crisis that forces flight or arrest

acts.title: The Heretic's Brand
acts.description: Joe faces interrogation or trial, where his modern knowledge and strange possessions mark him as either a witch or foreign spy. He must navigate Tudor justice while building unlikely alliances and discovering others who share supernatural secrets.
acts.end_condition: Joe escapes custody or is sentenced to burn, forcing a final confrontation
locked_constraints: Joe cannot return to his own time
The full supernatural truth remains hidden
key_beats: Modern items examined as evidence of witchcraft

acts.title: Fire and Brimstone
acts.description: In a desperate finale, Joe must choose between exposing the supernatural truth to save himself or protecting others at great personal cost. His modern knowledge becomes both salvation and curse as he faces the ultimate test of survival in Tudor England.
acts.end_condition: Joe either returns to his time or dies in the attempt
key_beats: Choice between self-preservation and protecting others

npcs.name: Brother Francis
npcs.motivation: Torn between religious duty and growing sympathy for Joe's plight
npcs.secret: Secretly studies forbidden texts and questions Church doctrine

npcs.name: Mary Blackthorne
npcs.motivation: Seeking to protect others with supernatural secrets from persecution
npcs.secret: Is actually a time-displaced person from the 18th century who has been trapped for decades

npcs.name: Magistrate Edmund Blackwood

npcs.secret: Uses his position to eliminate personal enemies by branding them as heretics

npcs.name: Tom the Cutpurse

npcs.secret: Has connections to both criminals and Church officials

factions.name: The Church
factions.description: The powerful religious establishment seeking to root out heresy and maintain orthodoxy in Henry VIII's reformed England
factions.goals: Identify and eliminate supernatural threats while maintaining religious and social order

factions.name: The Hermetic Circle
factions.description: A secret society of scholars and mystics who study forbidden knowledge and protect those touched by supernatural forces
factions.goals: Preserve ancient knowledge and protect supernatural individuals from persecution

world_rules: Time displacement is real but extremely rare and poorly understood
Modern technology appears as witchcraft to Tudor minds
The Church actively hunts suspected heretics and witches
Social class and proper behavior are rigidly enforced
Disease, violence, and execution are constant threats
Supernatural knowledge exists but is heavily persecuted

data.passage:
Your skull feels like it's been used as a battering ram by a particularly vindictive troll. The taste in your mouth suggests something crawled in there to die—probably your dignity. You crack open one bloodshot eye and immediately regret it as watery sunlight stabs through your retinas like divine retribution.

This isn't your apartment. Hell, this isn't even a proper alley. You're sprawled against rough stone walls between buildings that look like they were built by someone who'd only heard architecture described secondhand. The smell hits you next—a nauseating cocktail of human waste, rotting fish, and despair that makes your hangover seem quaint by comparison.

Your jeans are soaked through with something you desperately hope is just water. Your iPhone is gone, but somehow three tarnished coins have materialized in your pocket. The people shuffling past the alley mouth are wearing what looks like Renaissance fair costumes, except they're covered in genuine medieval grime.

A woman hurries by carrying a wooden bucket, her dress trailing in the muck. She glances at you with a mixture of fear and disgust before crossing herself and muttering what sounds suspiciously like Latin. Your credit cards feel utterly useless as you watch a man drive a cart pulled by an actual ox down what should be a London street.

Something is very, very wrong here.

A.text: Approach the woman with the bucket and ask where you are
A.consequence: The woman reacts with fear to Joe's strange appearance and modern speech, potentially drawing unwanted attention from authorities
A.narration_directive: Show the woman's terror at Joe's foreign appearance and strange clothing, have her cry out for help or flee, attracting the attention of local Watch or other authorities

B.text: Check your pockets more thoroughly and try to piece together what happened
B.consequence: Joe discovers more about his situation without drawing attention, finding clues about how he got here
B.narration_directive: Let Joe examine his possessions more carefully, discovering the mysterious nature of the three shillings and getting a better sense of his predicament without alerting anyone

C.text: Stand up and follow the ox cart to see where it leads
C.consequence: Following the cart leads Joe into a crowded market area where his strange appearance draws hostile attention from merchants and customers
C.narration_directive: Have Joe follow the cart into a busy Tudor marketplace where his modern clothes and confused behavior make him stand out dangerously among the locals

D.text: Stay hidden in the alley and observe the strange people passing by
D.consequence: Joe observes the Tudor world safely, learning about the time period and local customs without exposing himself
D.narration_directive: Allow Joe to watch and learn about Tudor London from the shadows, picking up details about the year, customs, and dangers while remaining hidden

- proximity_to_climax: REQUIRED every turn — set this value in state_updates using the formula in the PACING section below
- event_log_entry is required — always summarize what happened this turn

Do not create entries for one-off background characters (shopkeepers, random guards, etc.).

- The current act's target_scenes (in the skeleton) is the intended scene count for this act.
- Compute scenes elapsed in this act: scene_number - act_start_scene + 1 (both values are in CURRENT POSITION above).
- Set proximity_to_climax = (scenes_in_act / target_scenes), clamped to [0.0, 1.0]. Include this in state_updates EVERY turn.

- status_effects is the FULL current list every turn (replace semantics). Include ALL active effects, adjusting severity and descriptions as appropriate.

Example: a stab wound becomes { id: "stab_wound_001", name: "Stab Wound", description: "A deep cut in your side that bleeds freely", severity: 0.8, time_remaining: { days: 5, hours: 0, minutes: 0, seconds: 0 }, lethal: false }.
- Adjust severity each turn based on context: resting lowers severity, aggravation raises it, medicine/magic accelerates healing.
- Severity scale: 1.0 = completely debilitating, 0.7 = severely impaired, 0.5 = significant but manageable, 0.3 = noticeable nuisance, 0.1 = almost healed.
- time_remaining can be null for effects with no natural expiry (e.g. a curse that requires a specific action to remove).
- removal_condition can be set for effects that need specific actions (e.g. "Find the Witch of the Moor to break this curse"). Can be combined with time_remaining.
- Healing times should be genre/setting-appropriate. Consult the HEALING CONTEXT above. Realistic settings = realistic healing (broken bone: weeks, minor cut: days). Fantasy/sci-fi = faster if the setting provides healing methods.

PENDING CONSEQUENCES:
- New consequences use time_remaining ({ days, hours, minutes, seconds }) instead of scenes_remaining. Estimate how much in-game time before the consequence triggers.
- The client ticks down consequence time_remaining by time_elapsed each turn.

- choice_metadata MUST include outcome, consequence, and narration_directive for every choice
- Maintain safe_choice_ratio: approximately 0.35 of choices should be advance_safe
- Lethal status effects are allowed but MUST be foreshadowed. If an effect will kill the player, there should have been clues in earlier passages.

- Full severity range (0.0-1.0). Injuries are serious and heal realistically.
- Pending consequences escalate fast: short time windows before they trigger.
- NPCs have low forgiveness. Burning a relationship has lasting consequences.
- Include at least one advance_risky or severe_penalty outcome per set of choices.

Your skull feels like it's been used as a battering ram by a particularly vindictive troll. The taste in your mouth suggests something crawled in there to die—probably your dignity. You crack open one bloodshot eye and immediately regret it as watery sunlight stabs through your retinas like divine retribution.

This isn't your apartment. Hell, this isn't even a proper alley. You're sprawled against rough stone walls between buildings that look like they were built by someone who'd only heard architecture described secondhand. The smell hits you next—a nauseating cocktail of human waste, rotting fish, and despair that makes your hangover seem quaint by comparison.

Your jeans are soaked through with something you desperately hope is just water. Your iPhone is gone, but somehow three tarnished coins have materialized in your pocket. The people shuffling past the alley mouth are wearing what looks like Renaissance fair costumes, except they're covered in genuine medieval grime.

A woman hurries by carrying a wooden bucket, her dress trailing in the muck. She glances at you with a mixture of fear and disgust before crossing herself and muttering what sounds suspiciously like Latin. Your credit cards feel utterly useless as you watch a man drive a cart pulled by an actual ox down what should be a London street.

Something is very, very wrong here.

A: Approach the woman with the bucket and ask where you are

C: Stand up and follow the ox cart to see where it leads
D: Stay hidden in the alley and observe the strange people passing by

data.passage:
You lean against the grimy brick wall, head still throbbing like a bass drum in a cathedral, and methodically go through your pockets. Your jeans feel wrong against your skin—too clean, too modern for this place that reeks of horse piss and rotting cabbage. The credit cards in your wallet might as well be playing cards for all the good they'll do you here, and your driver's license photo stares back with the smug confidence of a man who thought his biggest problem would be finding an Uber home.

But it's the three silver coins that make your stomach drop. They're not tourist replicas or costume jewelry—they're heavy, tarnished, and bear the unmistakable profile of a bearded king. Henry VIII, if your half-remembered history serves. The metal feels warm against your palm, as if they've been waiting in your pocket for centuries.

A church bell tolls somewhere in the distance, its bronze voice echoing off timber and stone. Your smartphone remains stubbornly dead, a black mirror that reflects your growing panic. The alley stretches before you like a medieval fever dream, complete with the distant sound of merchants hawking their wares and the occasional splash of something unspeakable being emptied from an upper window.

Your hangover pounds with renewed vigor, but beneath the alcohol fog, a terrible clarity emerges: this isn't a dream, and you're not going home anytime soon.

A.text: Head toward the sound of merchants and try to blend in with the crowd
A.consequence: Joe attracts unwanted attention from merchants and locals who notice his strange clothes and behavior
A.narration_directive: Have Joe enter the marketplace but struggle with the language and customs, drawing suspicious looks and questions from vendors

B.text: Look for someone who might speak recognizable English and ask for directions
B.consequence: Joe finds a helpful local who can communicate and provides basic information about the area
B.narration_directive: Let Joe encounter a reasonably educated person who can speak in a way he understands and give him basic orientation

C.text: Find a quiet spot to observe and listen before making any moves
C.consequence: Joe learns valuable information about Tudor society and customs through careful observation
C.narration_directive: Allow Joe to observe and learn about daily life, social hierarchies, and potential dangers without being noticed

D.text: Examine the buildings more closely for clues about exactly when and where you are
D.consequence: Joe's examination of buildings leads him to trespass or behave suspiciously, attracting the attention of the Watch
D.narration_directive: Have Joe's investigation draw the attention of local authorities who become suspicious of his strange behavior and appearance

You lean against the grimy brick wall, head still throbbing like a bass drum in a cathedral, and methodically go through your pockets. Your jeans feel wrong against your skin—too clean, too modern for this place that reeks of horse piss and rotting cabbage. The credit cards in your wallet might as well be playing cards for all the good they'll do you here, and your driver's license photo stares back with the smug confidence of a man who thought his biggest problem would be finding an Uber home.

But it's the three silver coins that make your stomach drop. They're not tourist replicas or costume jewelry—they're heavy, tarnished, and bear the unmistakable profile of a bearded king. Henry VIII, if your half-remembered history serves. The metal feels warm against your palm, as if they've been waiting in your pocket for centuries.

A church bell tolls somewhere in the distance, its bronze voice echoing off timber and stone. Your smartphone remains stubbornly dead, a black mirror that reflects your growing panic. The alley stretches before you like a medieval fever dream, complete with the distant sound of merchants hawking their wares and the occasional splash of something unspeakable being emptied from an upper window.

Your hangover pounds with renewed vigor, but beneath the alcohol fog, a terrible clarity emerges: this isn't a dream, and you're not going home anytime soon.

data.passage:
You stumble toward the cacophony of voices, your sneakers squelching through mud that smells like a cocktail of horse piss and rotting vegetables. The hangover pounds behind your eyes like an angry drunk with a sledgehammer, but the sight ahead cuts through the alcoholic fog like a slap to the face.

The marketplace sprawls before you in a riot of medieval chaos. Vendors hawk their wares from rickety wooden stalls, their voices rising in a strange sing-song cadence that sounds like English but feels foreign on your ears. "Fresh eels! Finest eels from the Thames!" one cries, while another bellows about "good ale, not three days sour!"

You try to blend in, pulling your jacket closer, but it's like trying to hide a neon sign in a candlelit cathedral. A fishmonger's eyes lock onto your sneakers—those pristine white Nikes might as well be glowing. "What manner of footwear be those, good sir?" he calls out, his voice carrying too far.

Other heads turn. A woman selling bread squints at your jeans with suspicion. "Foreign cloth," she mutters to her neighbor. "Mark how strangely 'tis woven."

A small crowd begins to gather, their medieval curiosity piqued by your obviously alien appearance. The attention feels dangerous, like standing in a spotlight when you should be hiding in shadows.

A.text: Claim you're a traveling merchant from distant lands to explain your strange clothes
A.consequence: Joe's story about being a foreign merchant draws more questions about his origins and trade goods, potentially making things worse
A.narration_directive: Have the merchants press Joe for details about what lands he comes from and what goods he trades, with his answers creating more suspicion

B.text: Pretend to be mute and gesture that you cannot speak
B.consequence: Pretending to be mute reduces immediate questioning but may attract different kinds of attention
B.narration_directive: Show Joe successfully avoiding verbal interrogation through gestures, though some locals remain curious about his appearance

C.text: Quickly duck into the nearest tavern to escape the growing attention
C.consequence: Fleeing to a tavern escapes immediate attention but creates new problems in an enclosed space with different people
C.narration_directive: Have Joe escape the marketplace crowd but find himself trapped in a tavern with patrons who also notice his strange appearance

D.text: Try to buy something with your Tudor coins to prove you belong here
D.consequence: Using Tudor coins while dressed strangely raises serious questions about how Joe acquired authentic currency, potentially marking him as a thief
D.narration_directive: Show the merchants becoming deeply suspicious about how a strangely-dressed foreigner has genuine English coins, with accusations of theft beginning

data.passage: You fish out one of the silver coins, its weight reassuringly real in your palm. "Here, good sir," you say to the baker, holding up the shilling. "I'd like to purchase some bread." The man's eyes widen as he examines the coin, his flour-dusted fingers turning it over with practiced expertise. The crowd presses closer, their murmurs growing darker. "This be genuine English silver," the baker announces, his voice carrying a dangerous edge. "Minted not two years past." A woman in a stained apron points an accusing finger. "Look at his garb! No honest Englishman dresses in such foreign weaves!" Another voice pipes up: "Aye, and see how pale he is? Like he's never done honest work!" Your head throbs as the implications crash down on you. In their minds, you're either a thief who's robbed some poor soul, or worse—a foreign spy with suspiciously perfect English currency. The baker's expression has shifted from confusion to cold suspicion. "Tell me, stranger," he says slowly, "how does a man dressed like a vagabond from distant lands come by fresh-minted Tudor silver?" The crowd tightens around you like a noose, their faces hard with growing certainty that they've caught a criminal red-handed.

B.narration_directive: The crowd becomes more aggressive, viewing this as an obvious lie, with someone calling for the Watch

C.text: Try to push through the crowd and escape before this gets worse
C.consequence: Attempting to flee will confirm their suspicions but might allow escape if successful
C.narration_directive: Joe tries to push through the crowd but this triggers shouts of 'thief' and pursuit, creating a chase scene

D.text: Demand to speak with someone in authority to clear up this misunderstanding
D.consequence: Demanding authority shows confidence and might make them hesitate, though it brings official attention
D.narration_directive: Joe's bold demand for authority surprises the crowd and makes them pause, though someone mentions fetching the Watch

data.passage:
You lower your shoulder and push into the crowd, desperation overriding caution. The moment you move, the marketplace erupts. "Stop him!" bellows the baker, his face purple with righteous fury. "Thief! He's getting away!" The word spreads like wildfire through the packed square, and suddenly every face turns toward you with predatory interest.

Your sneakers slip on the slick cobblestones as you shove past a woman carrying a basket of turnips. She shrieks and tumbles backward, vegetables scattering everywhere. Behind you, heavy boots pound against stone as half the marketplace gives chase. Your hangover pounds in rhythm with your racing heart, each step sending spikes of pain through your skull.

You dart between wooden stalls, knocking over displays of pottery and dried fish. Angry shouts echo off the timber-framed buildings that lean over the narrow streets like disapproving giants. A butcher's apprentice lunges for you with bloody hands, but you twist away, your modern clothes giving you just enough speed advantage.

Ahead, the street forks into three directions: a narrow alley that disappears into shadow, a wider road leading toward what looks like a church spire, and a path sloping down toward the Thames where you can smell the river's fetid breath mixing with your own panic-sweat.

A.text: Duck into the narrow alley and hope to lose them in the maze of side streets
A.consequence: The alley might provide cover but could be a dead end or contain other dangers
A.narration_directive: Joe enters the narrow alley but discovers it's a maze where he could get lost or trapped, with pursuit still close behind

B.text: Head toward the church - surely they won't pursue you into sanctuary
B.consequence: Sanctuary law might protect Joe, and the pursuers may hesitate to violate church grounds
B.narration_directive: Joe reaches the church and claims sanctuary, forcing his pursuers to stop at the threshold while he catches his breath

C.text: Run toward the Thames and try to find a boat or hiding place near the docks
C.consequence: The docks offer hiding places but are full of criminals and cutthroats who might turn Joe in for a reward
C.narration_directive: Joe reaches the Thames docks where he encounters dock workers and river folk who may help or hinder his escape

D.text: Turn and face your pursuers, trying to explain yourself before they catch you
D.consequence: Stopping to explain will result in capture by the angry mob and likely violence before any explanation can be heard
D.narration_directive: Joe is overwhelmed by the pursuing crowd who tackle him to the ground, leading to rough handling and potential injury

data.passage:
You stumble through the heavy oak doors of St. Bartholomew's just as your legs threaten to give out entirely. The cool stone interior hits you like a blessed reprieve from the chaos outside, and you collapse against a pillar, gasping. Your head feels like someone's taken a mallet to it, each heartbeat sending fresh waves of agony through your skull.

'Sanctuary!' you wheeze, though you're not entirely sure that's how it works.

Outside, the angry voices of your pursuers grow louder, then suddenly stop at the threshold. Through the doorway, you see a cluster of red-faced merchants and apprentices gesticulating wildly, but none dare cross into the sacred space. One particularly irate baker shakes his fist at you. 'Come out, you thieving devil! The Watch will hear of this!'

A figure emerges from the shadows near the altar—a young monk in rough brown robes, his tonsured head gleaming in the candlelight. Brother Francis, according to the wooden nameplate on his reading desk. He approaches cautiously, taking in your strange attire with obvious concern.

'Peace, good sir,' he says softly, his voice carrying the educated accent of someone born to better circumstances. 'What brings you to seek sanctuary in such... unusual garb?' His eyes linger on your sneakers with barely concealed fascination. 'And why do they name you thief?'

A.text: Tell him you're a traveler from a distant land and were falsely accused

A.narration_directive: Brother Francis listens politely but his educated mind picks up on inconsistencies in Joe's story, leading to more probing questions about his 'distant land'

B.text: Claim you're a merchant whose foreign coins were mistaken for stolen goods
B.consequence: A plausible explanation that Brother Francis can accept, though he remains curious about Joe's strange clothes
B.narration_directive: Brother Francis nods thoughtfully at the merchant explanation, though he remains puzzled by Joe's foreign attire and asks about his trade goods

C.text: Say you're fleeing religious persecution and need the Church's protection
C.consequence: Claiming religious persecution will trigger Brother Francis's duty to investigate potential heresy, creating dangerous scrutiny
C.narration_directive: Brother Francis's demeanor shifts to serious concern as religious persecution implies heresy - he must now determine if Joe is a victim or the heretic himself

D.text: Remain silent and let him draw his own conclusions while you recover
D.consequence: Silence will seem suspicious and evasive, making Brother Francis more wary while the crowd outside grows restless
D.narration_directive: Joe's silence makes Brother Francis increasingly uncomfortable, and the angry voices outside grow louder as the crowd debates whether to fetch the Watch

data.passage:
Brother Francis nods slowly, his weathered face creasing in thought as he considers your words. "A merchant, you say?" His voice carries the measured tone of a man accustomed to hearing confessions both true and false. "Foreign coins would indeed cause confusion among the common folk. They see silver they do not recognize and cry 'thief' before asking questions."

He steps closer, his brown robes rustling against the stone floor. The sanctuary's cool air provides blessed relief from your pounding headache, though the sound of angry voices still echoes faintly from beyond the church doors. "Yet I confess, good merchant, your garments puzzle me greatly. This cloth..." He reaches toward your cotton t-shirt with curious fingers. "'Tis woven like nothing I have seen, and these shoes... from what land do you hail that produces such strange footwear?"

Outside, you hear the shuffle of feet and murmured conversations. The crowd hasn't dispersed entirely—some linger, waiting to see if you'll emerge. Brother Francis notices your glance toward the door. "Sanctuary protects you for now, but it is not eternal. Eventually, you must venture forth. And when you do..." He pauses meaningfully. "Perhaps it would be wise to have more than strange tales to protect you. The Watch grows suspicious of foreigners these days, especially those who cannot account for their presence properly."

A.text: Ask Brother Francis if he can provide you with proper English clothes to blend in
A.consequence: Brother Francis agrees to help find suitable clothing, improving your disguise and relationship with him
A.narration_directive: Brother Francis considers the request sympathetically and offers to help, perhaps from the church's charity stores or through his connections

B.text: Inquire about safe passage out of London before the Watch arrives
B.consequence: Brother Francis reveals concerning information about increased Watch patrols and checkpoints, creating new obstacles
B.narration_directive: Brother Francis explains the dangers of leaving London now, mentioning specific Watch activities that complicate escape

C.text: Tell him you lost your trade goods and documentation in a robbery
C.consequence: The robbery story raises more questions about your lack of injuries and creates inconsistencies in your merchant tale
C.narration_directive: Brother Francis becomes more suspicious as he notices flaws in the robbery explanation, pressing for more details

D.text: Ask what kind of 'accounting' the Watch would require from a foreign merchant
D.consequence: Brother Francis reveals the harsh reality of Tudor documentation requirements, making your situation seem nearly hopeless
D.narration_directive: Brother Francis explains the strict requirements for foreign merchants, including papers you cannot possibly have, making him realize how precarious your situation truly is

data.passage:
Brother Francis strokes his chin thoughtfully, his eyes taking in your peculiar garments with a mixture of curiosity and concern. "Aye, that would be wise indeed," he murmurs, glancing toward the heavy oak doors where angry voices still echo from the courtyard. "Your... foreign attire draws unwanted attention, as we've witnessed."

He moves to a wooden chest beneath a faded tapestry depicting Saint Bartholomew's martyrdom. "The church keeps clothing for the destitute and pilgrims who arrive with naught but rags." His fingers work at the iron clasps. "'Tis not fine cloth, but it will serve to make you appear less... conspicuous."

Your head throbs less fiercely in the cool sanctuary air, though the memory of the hostile crowd makes your stomach churn. Brother Francis withdraws a rough woolen doublet, brown hose, and a simple cap. "These should fit well enough. There's a small chamber behind the altar where you may change in privacy."

He pauses, studying you with those intelligent eyes. "I confess, good merchant, your speech carries strange inflections, and your manner is... unusual. The Watch grows ever more suspicious of foreigners, especially those who cannot account for their presence in London. Perhaps while you don these proper garments, you might consider what tale you'll tell should they question you further?"

The sound of boots on cobblestones echoes from outside—official, measured steps that make Brother Francis stiffen.

A.consequence: Joe successfully changes clothes and learns valuable information about Watch procedures, improving his chances of avoiding detection
A.narration_directive: Brother Francis provides helpful details about how the Watch operates while Joe changes into convincing Tudor attire

B.consequence: Brother Francis reveals there is a back exit but warns it leads directly into Watch patrol routes - attempting escape could trigger immediate pursuit
B.narration_directive: Brother Francis shows Joe the back exit but explains the dangerous timing with Watch patrols, creating a tense decision point

C.text: Confide partially in Brother Francis about your true circumstances, hoping to gain his trust
C.consequence: Partial confession about time displacement causes Brother Francis to suspect witchcraft, potentially turning him from ally to threat
C.narration_directive: Brother Francis reacts with growing alarm and suspicion as Joe's partial truth sounds like heretical claims, threatening their fragile alliance

D.text: Hide your modern clothes but keep them with you while changing, in case you need them later
D.consequence: Keeping modern clothes creates a hidden cache but risks discovery if searched - adds both opportunity and danger
D.narration_directive: Joe successfully hides his modern clothes but Brother Francis notices the suspicious behavior, creating tension about what Joe is concealing

Brother Francis strokes his chin thoughtfully, his eyes taking in your peculiar garments with a mixture of curiosity and concern. "Aye, that would be wise indeed," he murmurs, glancing toward the heavy oak doors where angry voices still echo from the courtyard. "Your... foreign attire draws unwanted attention, as we've witnessed."

He moves to a wooden chest beneath a faded tapestry depicting Saint Bartholomew's martyrdom. "The church keeps clothing for the destitute and pilgrims who arrive with naught but rags." His fingers work at the iron clasps. "'Tis not fine cloth, but it will serve to make you appear less... conspicuous."

Your head throbs less fiercely in the cool sanctuary air, though the memory of the hostile crowd makes your stomach churn. Brother Francis withdraws a rough woolen doublet, brown hose, and a simple cap. "These should fit well enough. There's a small chamber behind the altar where you may change in privacy."

He pauses, studying you with those intelligent eyes. "I confess, good merchant, your speech carries strange inflections, and your manner is... unusual. The Watch grows ever more suspicious of foreigners, especially those who cannot account for their presence in London. Perhaps while you don these proper garments, you might consider what tale you'll tell should they question you further?"

The sound of boots on cobblestones echoes from outside—official, measured steps that make Brother Francis stiffen.

data.passage:
Brother Francis turns his back respectfully as you strip out of your jeans and t-shirt, the rough wool and linen of the Tudor garments scratching against your skin like medieval sandpaper. The doublet is too tight across your chest, the hose sag comically at your ankles, and the codpiece... well, that's an adjustment you never thought you'd have to make.

"The Watch operates in shifts," Francis explains quietly, his voice echoing off the stone walls. "They change at dawn, noon, and dusk. Captain Aldrich leads the day patrol—a brutal man who believes a good beating prevents crime better than prayer." He glances nervously toward the door. "They have authority to arrest on suspicion alone, especially foreigners. Your accent... it marks you as clearly as a brand."

You struggle with the leather jerkin, your hangover making your fingers clumsy. The crowd's angry murmur still drifts through the sanctuary doors like a brewing storm.

"There's more," Francis continues, wringing his hands. "Magistrate Blackwood has been... zealous lately. Three men burned for heresy this month alone. He claims the Devil walks among us in strange guises." His eyes flick meaningfully to your discarded modern clothes. "Perhaps it would be wise if those garments... disappeared."

Outside, you hear the distinct sound of hobnailed boots on cobblestones. The Watch approaches.

B.narration_directive: The burning clothes create suspicious smoke that alerts the Watch to activity inside the sanctuary

C.text: Keep your modern clothes hidden on your person and slip out the back of the church
C.consequence: Attempting to hide modern items on your person risks discovery during a search, and fleeing sanctuary breaks Church law
C.narration_directive: Joe's attempt to smuggle evidence while fleeing puts him in maximum danger from both Church and secular law

D.text: Confront the situation head-on and walk out to meet the Watch directly
D.consequence: Walking directly into the Watch's hands while they're specifically hunting for a thief leads to immediate arrest and likely execution
D.narration_directive: Joe's direct confrontation with the Watch results in immediate capture and a swift path to the gallows
